changes_summary: Addressed all three open flags. FLAG-001: Reworked `PRODUCER_FLOWS` so `test_confirmed_by` / `real_confirmed_by` are frozensets of `PaymentActorKind` (not single values), and set `admin_chat.real_confirmed_by = {RECIPIENT_CLICK, RECIPIENT_MESSAGE}` to represent both current confirmation paths. `_authorize_actor` logic now checks set membership. FLAG-002: Added Phase 3 Step 9 subtask to retarget `main.py:65-77 _bind_cap_breach_dm` from `get_cog('PaymentCog')` to `get_cog('PaymentWorkerCog')`, and added explicit grep for other `get_cog('PaymentCog')` references. Added a dedicated test case in Step 12.4 asserting the cap-breach wiring resolves to the worker cog. FLAG-004: Gave `safe_delete_messages` a concrete, tested caller in `PaymentWorkerCog._handle_terminal_payment` against `payment.metadata.cleanup_message_ids` (empty today = no-op, populated by the future admin-DM feature). Added `tests/test_terminal_cleanup_wiring.py` to prove the integration and the no-behavior-change claim. Reordered the execution plan so Step 10 lands before Step 7 (worker cog must import the helper). Overview and success criteria updated to reference the new wiring.
questions: Does the admin-DM feature megaplan plan to populate `metadata.cleanup_message_ids` when it creates payments? The Step 10 wiring assumes yes; if no, the primitive is live but dead-code until someone populates it.
Is there a preferred name for the metadata key (`cleanup_message_ids` vs `thread_cleanup_message_ids`)? I'll default to `cleanup_message_ids` unless you have a prior convention.

Should `safe_delete_messages` also be wired into the grants terminal path (`grants_cog._handle_final_payment_result` at grants_cog.py:749-762) to delete the intermediate confirmation button before archiving the thread? That would change behavior (currently the button message stays in the archived thread). Default: no — out of scope for behavior preservation.
Is `tests/test_terminal_cleanup_wiring.py` acceptable as a new test file, or would you prefer the assertion be folded into `tests/test_payment_cog_split.py`?

success_criteria.criterion: `PaymentService.confirm_payment` accepts exactly one well-typed entrypoint: `(payment_id, *, actor: PaymentActor, guild_id=None)`. No `privileged_override`, `confirmed_by`, or `confirmed_by_user_id` parameters remain.
`grep -r 'privileged_override' src/ tests/` returns no matches.

All four call sites (PaymentConfirmView in payment_ui_cog, admin_chat _handle_confirmation_received, grants_cog auto-confirm, admin_chat auto-confirm) pass a `PaymentActor` instead of string/bool kwargs.

`PRODUCER_FLOWS` registry exists at `src/features/payments/producer_flows.py`. `test_confirmed_by` and `real_confirmed_by` are `frozenset[PaymentActorKind]`. `grants` = `({AUTO}, {RECIPIENT_CLICK})`. `admin_chat` = `({AUTO}, {RECIPIENT_CLICK, RECIPIENT_MESSAGE})` so the button and free-text paths both confirm.

`PaymentService._authorize_actor` reads `PRODUCER_FLOWS` for every confirmation, uses set membership, and preserves the hardening null-fail-closed for recipient-kind actors (null recipient_discord_id or null actor_id both reject).
Unknown producer passed to `get_flow` results in the payment being rejected (fail-closed) and logged at WARNING.

`payment_requests` schema has an additive `tx_signature_history jsonb not null default '[]'::jsonb` column; existing rows are backfilled with a single-entry history where `tx_signature is not null`. The RLS preamble at sql/payments.sql:1-7 is untouched.
`requeue_payment` appends a history entry capturing the prior signature before blanking `tx_signature`. Verified by `tests/test_tx_signature_history.py`.
`PaymentCog` is deleted; `PaymentWorkerCog` and `PaymentUICog` are both registered in `main.py` and load without errors.

`main.py:65-77 _bind_cap_breach_dm` (and any other `get_cog('PaymentCog')` lookup in the repo) is retargeted to the correct new cog. A cap-breach event still results in `_dm_admin_payment_failure` firing on `PaymentWorkerCog`. Verified by `tests/test_payment_cog_split.py`.

Admin success-DM env vars and conditional branch in `_handle_terminal_payment` are preserved on `PaymentWorkerCog` exactly as they were on `PaymentCog` — no regression in the hardening success-DM threshold behavior.

On restart, persistent confirmation views for `pending_confirmation` payments are re-registered exactly once via `PaymentUICog.cog_load`. Verified by `tests/test_payment_cog_split.py` asserting `bot.add_view` was called.

`safe_delete_messages` exists in `src/common/discord_utils.py`, handles NotFound/Forbidden/HTTPException(429)/generic HTTPException/None-channel without raising, and is invoked from `PaymentWorkerCog._handle_terminal_payment` against `payment.metadata.cleanup_message_ids`. Verified by `tests/test_safe_delete_messages.py` and `tests/test_terminal_cleanup_wiring.py`.
Existing tests in `tests/test_admin_payments.py` and `tests/test_caller_paths.py` pass (with only the mechanical migration to `PaymentActor` and updated cog names in fakes).
Full `pytest` run is green.

No user-visible behavior change in grants flow (auto-confirm on test, recipient click on real), current admin_chat flow (auto-confirm on test, either button OR free-text on real), cap-breach DM, or admin success-DM threshold. Any deviation is explicitly called out in the PR as scope creep.
`PaymentWorkerCog` and `PaymentUICog` are each under ~400 lines (the current combined file is 576). Neither holds both a `discord.ui.View` class and the worker loop.
`PaymentService._authorize_actor` has a single code path per `PaymentActorKind` value — no nested `if privileged_override else` branches.
`tx_signature_history` entries include `signature`, `status`, `timestamp`, `reason`, `send_phase` — consistent shape across submit/confirm/requeue/backfill writes.
Manual smoke: start the bot against a test guild; run one grants test payout (auto-confirm) and one real grants payout (recipient clicks button) and confirm both complete.
Manual smoke: restart the bot with a payment in `pending_confirmation` and confirm the recipient can still click the button on the pre-restart message.
Manual smoke: trigger a cap-breach manual-hold and verify the admin still receives the DM (proves the Step 9 `_bind_cap_breach_dm` retarget is live).
Manual smoke: in the admin_chat flow, confirm a final payout via the button in the payment channel AND (separately) via a free-text recipient reply in the admin chat — both must still succeed.

assumptions: `PRODUCER_FLOWS` drives authorization only in this plan — `thread_cleanup` and `notify_admin_dm_on_approval` fields exist on the dataclass but are NOT read. The admin-DM feature will consume them. Reading them here would be a behavior change.

`admin_chat.real_confirmed_by = {RECIPIENT_CLICK, RECIPIENT_MESSAGE}` is the exact current behavior: both the button path (admin_chat_cog.py:389-402) and the free-text path (admin_chat_cog.py:284-309) confirm the same final payment row today, and both must continue to work.
`admin_chat.test_confirmed_by = {AUTO}` matches current behavior at admin_chat_cog.py:269-274. The brief's future-state `{RECIPIENT_CLICK}` is the admin-DM feature's job.

`PaymentActorKind` subclasses `str` so `confirmed_by` column values become `recipient_click`/`recipient_message`/`auto`/`admin_dm`. This is a log/DB-string rename; historical rows keep their old strings (`discord`/`auto`/`free_text`/`user`); nothing reads those values externally.
`tx_signature_history` is audit-only. No code reads it back.

`safe_delete_messages` is wired from `PaymentWorkerCog._handle_terminal_payment` via `payment.metadata.cleanup_message_ids`, which is empty for every current producer — so today the call is a verified no-op. The admin-DM feature populates the list when it creates payments. This satisfies the 'used by the thread cleanup path' success criterion via a real integration with test coverage, not a dangling primitive.

`main.py` is the single cog-wiring point. `_bind_cap_breach_dm` at main.py:65-77 is the only cap-breach callback resolver and must be retargeted to `PaymentWorkerCog`. Any additional `get_cog('PaymentCog')` references found during Step 9 grep are updated in the same change.
Tests use the in-memory fake `db_handler` for `tx_signature_history`; Supabase is not spun up in the test suite.
`PaymentCog` is deleted outright with no compat shim. All imports and cog lookups are updated in the same change.
